text: He had never seen such a beautiful sunrise over the mountains, and he stood there for a long time watching the colors change from deep purple to gold.
The old professor adjusted his spectacles and began to read aloud from the yellowed pages of the ancient manuscript that had been discovered in the library's basement.
She walked briskly through the crowded marketplace, past vendors selling spices and silks, looking for the small bookshop her grandmother had told her about.
It was a truth universally acknowledged that a single man in possession of a good fortune must be in want of a wife.
The laboratory was silent except for the gentle hum of the centrifuge and the occasional beep of the monitoring equipment on the far bench.
After three days of continuous rain, the river had risen dangerously close to the top of the levee, and the townspeople gathered sandbags along the bank.
The children gathered around the fire, their faces glowing in the warm light, as their grandfather began to tell the story of the great voyage across the sea.
In the distance, the cathedral bells rang out across the sleeping city, marking the hour with their deep resonant tones that seemed to vibrate in the cold night air.

Philosophy begins in wonder, and at the end when philosophic thought has done its best the wonder remains. There have been added however some grasp of the immensity of things.
